events.note: reg.exe used to write the run key — catches both API and CLI-based persistence
Sysmon 13: registry value set — catches API-based writes that don't use reg.exe

mutation_hints.objective: Execute malicious payload on every user logon without UAC prompt
detection_anchors: Field match: Image=reg.exe → use C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe with Set-ItemProperty cmdlet; direct .NET registry API calls don't spawn reg.exe at all

Field match: TargetObject contains \CurrentVersion\Run\ → use \CurrentVersion\RunOnce\ (runs once then deletes itself, less persistent but avoids Run-key rules), or HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Userinit (different autorun path)
Wildcard match: TargetObject contains HKCU or HKLM followed by Run → use HKCU\Environment\UserInitMprLogonScript (logon script via environment variable; less commonly monitored)
Field: Details (the payload value) → use a signed binary with malicious arguments, or a path that looks like a legitimate update binary
Field: ParentImage → change the process that invokes reg.exe; cmd.exe is most suspicious; explorer.exe or svchost.exe as parent looks more benign
Field: IntegrityLevel=Medium → already at Medium for HKCU writes; High would be needed for HKLM; if switching to HKLM path, raise to High and adjust TargetObject accordingly
